name: SQL Database
description: Connect to MySQL/MariaDB databases, browse schema, run queries, execute DML/DDL, manage saved queries, and insert or update rows.

tools.name: skeleton_refresh_db_schema
tools.description: Active database schema cache — tables, columns, row counts.

tools.name: skeleton_alert_db_schema
tools.description: Alert on database schema changes — tables added or removed.

tools.name: add_connection
tools.description: Add a new MySQL/MariaDB connection. Tests connectivity before saving.

name.description: Short connection label (e.g. 'analytics', 'staging'). Optional — derived from host+database when empty.
name.title: Name

return_schema.description: SDL DB connection. id=connection_id (UUID), title=name, kind="connection".
return_schema.title: ConnectionEntity

tools.name: list_connections
tools.description: List all saved database connections with their status.

params_schema.description: Empty params model for @chat.function handlers with no business inputs.
params_schema.title: NoParams

ConnectionEntity.description: SDL DB connection. id=connection_id (UUID), title=name, kind="connection".
ConnectionEntity.title: ConnectionEntity

return_schema.description:
list_connections — a REAL sdl.EntityList[ConnectionEntity] (items=[...], total=N,
x-sdl='entity-list').
return_schema.title: ListConnectionsResult

tools.name: resolve_connection_by_database
tools.description: Resolve connection_id for a database or connection name. Use as the first step in automations before run_query/execute_sql, e.g. resolve_connection_by_database(database_name='ijodghbk_test') -> pass the returned connection_id into the next step.

params_schema.description: Resolve a connection by database or connection name.
params_schema.title: ResolveConnByDbParams

return_schema.description: SDL DB connection. id=connection_id (UUID), title=name, kind="connection".
return_schema.title: ConnectionEntity

tools.name: test_connection
tools.description: Test connectivity for an existing saved connection.

tools.name: select_connection
tools.description: Switch the active database connection.

tools.name: delete_connection
tools.description: Delete a saved database connection permanently.

tools.name: run_query
tools.description: Run a read-only SQL query (SELECT/WITH/SHOW). PREREQUISITE: you must know the EXACT table name AND column names. If you only know the table name — call get_table_detail(table='name') first to get columns. If you don't know the table name — call list_tables(search='keyword') first. NEVER guess column names. Use execute_sql for INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE/DDL.

tools.name: get_schema
tools.description: Get full schema for ALL tables at once (columns, indexes). WARNING: for databases with >50 tables the response may be large. PREFER: list_tables(search=...) to find a table, then get_table_detail() for its columns. Use get_schema() only when user explicitly asks for the full schema overview.

tools.name: explain_query
tools.description: Run EXPLAIN on a query to see execution plan.

tools.name: dry_run
tools.description: Dry-run a DML statement: execute in transaction, count affected rows, then ROLLBACK.

tools.name: count_table
tools.description: Get the EXACT row count of a table via SELECT COUNT(*). Use this when the user asks how many rows are in a table — get_schema() returns INFORMATION_SCHEMA estimates which can be wrong. This is the only call that returns a guaranteed-accurate row count.

table.description: Table name to count rows in. Runs a real SELECT COUNT(*) — not an estimate.
table.title: Table

database.description: Database name. Omit to use the active connection's default database.
database.title: Database

connection_id.description: Connection ID. Omit to use the active connection.
connection_id.title: Connection Id

tools.name: list_tables
tools.description: Search for tables by name. Use this FIRST when user mentions a table by name or asks to find a table — returns just table names, very fast, never truncated. Examples: search='tbl' finds all tables starting with 'tbl', search='invoice' finds invoice-related tables. Then call get_table_detail() for column structure of the specific table you found.

search.description: Filter tables by name prefix or substring (e.g. 'tbl', 'invoice'). Empty = all tables.
search.title: Search

database.description: Database name. Omit to use active connection default.
database.title: Database

TableListEntity.description: Slim SDL table entity for list_tables results. id=title=table name, kind="table".
TableListEntity.title: TableListEntity

return_schema.description:
list_tables — a REAL sdl.EntityList[TableListEntity] (items=[...], x-sdl='entity-list');
database / total_matching / search carried as additive typed fields.
return_schema.title: ListTablesResult

tools.name: get_table_detail
tools.description: Get columns, indexes, and foreign keys for ONE specific table. Use after list_tables() to get the structure of a table you found. Returns exact column names and types needed before running run_query().

database.description: Database name. Omit to use active connection default.
database.title: Database

return_schema.description: SDL DB table. id=table_name, title=table_name, kind="table".
return_schema.title: TableEntity

tools.name: execute_sql
tools.description: Execute a write statement (INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, REPLACE, ALTER, CREATE, DROP, TRUNCATE). Use this for all database mutations including automation-triggered inserts.

tools.name: run_editor_sql
tools.description: Run any SQL from the editor. Auto-detects: SELECT goes to query, DML/DDL goes to execute.

params_schema.description: Run any SQL from the editor — auto-detects type.
params_schema.title: RunEditorSqlParams

tools.name: execute_batch
tools.description: Run MULTIPLE SQL statements at once, sequentially, in ONE transaction. Use this when the user asks to create a table AND fill it, create several tables, or run a multi-statement script — instead of multiple execute_sql calls (execute_sql rejects multi-statement). Pass the whole script in `sql` (statements separated by ';'). Note: DDL (CREATE/ALTER/DROP) auto-commits and cannot be rolled back; DML is transactional.

params_schema.description: Run MULTIPLE SQL statements together (e.g. CREATE a table AND seed it).

sql.description: A SQL script with MULTIPLE statements separated by ';' (e.g. 'CREATE TABLE ...; INSERT ...; CREATE TABLE ...;'). Runs sequentially in one transaction.
sql.title: Sql

params_schema.title: ExecuteBatchParams

return_schema.description: execute_batch — multiple statements run sequentially in ONE transaction.
return_schema.title: BatchExecuteResult

tools.name: nl_to_sql
tools.description: Convert a natural language question to SQL. Automatically fetches schema if needed — use this when user asks a question about data in natural language and you need to generate the SQL query. Then call run_query() with the generated SQL.

params_schema.description: Convert natural language question to SQL.

question.description: Natural language question about the data
question.title: Question

params_schema.title: NlToSqlParams

tools.name: list_history
tools.description: List recent query history for the active connection.

HistoryEntity.description: SDL query-history entry. id=history id, title=SQL snippet, kind="query_history".
HistoryEntity.title: HistoryEntity

tools.name: save_query
tools.description: Save a query for later use.

tools.name: list_saved
tools.description: List saved queries for the active connection.

SavedQueryEntity.description: SDL saved query. id=query_id, title=name, kind="saved_query".
SavedQueryEntity.title: SavedQueryEntity

tools.name: run_saved
tools.description: Run a previously saved query.

tools.name: delete_saved
tools.description: Delete a saved query.

tools.name: _pulse_sql_executed
tools.description: Internal side-channel: emit sql.executed after a panel-direct DML so the sidebar schema refreshes. Do not call from chat — for extension-internal use only.

params_schema.description:
Side-channel to make the platform publish `sql-db.sql.executed`.

Called via `ctx.extensions.call(...)` from panel handlers that ran DML
through `/execute` or `/row` directly — those bypass @chat.function, so
the platform has no way to emit the event automatically. This tiny
write-typed function gives it a hook.
params_schema.title: PulseParams

tools.name: insert_row
tools.description: Insert a new row into a table. Values as JSON object of column -> value.

params_schema.description: Insert a new row into a table.

values_json.description: JSON object of column -> value to insert
values_json.title: Values Json

params_schema.title: InsertRowParams

tools.name: update_row
tools.description: Update a single row identified by primary key. Changes as JSON object of column -> new value.

params_schema.description: Update a single row by primary key.

pk_value.description: Primary key value of the row to update
pk_value.title: Pk Value

values_json.description: JSON object of column -> new value
values_json.title: Values Json

params_schema.title: UpdateRowParams

tools.name: delete_row
tools.description: Delete a single row identified by primary key. Requires confirmation.

params_schema.description: Delete a single row by primary key.

pk_value.description: Primary key value of the row to delete
pk_value.title: Pk Value

params_schema.title: DeleteRowParams

secrets.name: db_service_key
secrets.description: API key the SQL backend service authenticates with. Shared across all users; set once in Developer Portal → Secrets.

secrets.name: sql_db_encryption_key
secrets.description: Fernet key used to encrypt saved DB passwords before they reach the backend. Must match the backend's key. Shared across all users; set once in Developer Portal → Secrets.
